items.title: Just one year ago, the #Bucks were trying to extend a championship window around an all-time great.
items.body_excerpt:
Just one year ago, the #Bucks were trying to extend a championship window around an all-time great.

Now?

They just hope to be competitive, at some point, and then competitive on a yearly basis. 

A stark 180.

images.alt:
From a 404 article:

The data suggests, perhaps unsurprisingly, that a huge portion of longform posts on LinkedIn and X’s new article format are fully AI-generated or AI-assisted (meaning drafted, edited, or rewritten by AI with some human elements). Forty percent of longform LinkedIn posts analyzed in the data were fully AI-written; a quarter of X articles were fully AI written, but another 23 percent of X articles were AI-assisted, the company said. It intuitively makes sense that longer form content is more likely to be AI-generated, because people usually won’t bother to AI-generate a few word response or a pithy comment on a quote tweet, for example. AI is also famously verbose, meaning AI-generated content is more likely to show up in longer posts.

FanDuel’s intention, Thompson’s attorneys allege, was for Thompson to believe that Morgan was his friend.

items.title: My Zyon Pullin thoughts 💭
items.body_excerpt:
My Zyon Pullin thoughts 💭 

Glass ceiling comp: Explosive Ajay Mitchell
Boring mezzanine comp: Offensively skewed Tre Jones
Dark basement comp: Devin Carter

The Big Question: Can he prove himself to be a primary point guard and organize the team?

images.alt: Let’s not pull any punches about this. Zyon Pullin was one of the most dominant players in the G League last season. Had he not suffered a wrist injury that forced him out of action for a couple of months, he surely would’ve won more than just one Player of the Month award. Hell, in his first week back from a long absence, he won Player of the Week. If Pullin was healthy all season, Iowa would’ve been in significantly better playoff position instead of dropping down to face the finals-bound Stockton Kings in the first round.

So why is Zyon here?

Coach Bubes shared that he wants to see if Zyon can develop into a true point guard. Yes, he’s a reliably capable ball mover, but he more often opts to call his own number. Pullin is plenty good at that, but what else can he bring? I’m a big believer in him.

I think Zyon’s primed for a special summer league performance, especially after he inexplicably played his worst game of his G League season in a win-or-go home playoff game against the Kings two months ago.

Top-3 cap hits are highlighted for reference. 

The total % of the league cap the top three players take up is the bold valve to the left of the bar.

images.alt:
Horizontal stacked bar chart titled "How NBA Champions Spend the Cap," showing each player's percentage of the league salary cap for 11 championship teams. Bars are sorted from most recent (top) to oldest (bottom). The bold value is the combined cap percentage of the top three players. Teams and their top three:

Knicks (2025-26): 82.5% — Towns 34.36%, Anunoby 25.59%, Brunson 22.60%
Thunder (2024-25): 58.9% — Gilgeous-Alexander 25.51%, Hartenstein 21.34%, Dort 12.09%
Celtics (2023-24): 78.1% — Holiday 27.66%, Porzingis 26.48%, Tatum 23.97%
Nuggets (2022-23): 77.8% — Jokic 27.19%, Murray 25.60%, Porter Jr. 25.00%
Warriors (2021-22): 102.6% — Curry 40.73%, Thompson 33.79%, Wiggins 28.09%
Bucks (2020-21): 80.3% — Middleton 30.28%, Antetokounmpo 25.22%, Holiday 24.76%
Lakers (2019-20): 72.5% — James 34.30%, Davis 24.82%, Green 13.41%
Raptors (2018-19): 78.5% — Lowry 32.10%, Gasol 23.68%, Leonard 22.69%
Warriors (2017-18): 78.2% — Curry 35.00%, Durant 25.23%, Thompson 17.99%
Warriors (2016-17): 62.2% — Durant 28.19%, Thompson 17.70%, Green 16.28%

Data from Spotrac.

items.title: Victor Wembanyama locks himself into the 25% max (contract on the left), saving the Spurs $10 million annually should he qualify for the 30% max.
items.body_excerpt:
Victor Wembanyama locks himself into the 25% max (contract on the left), saving the Spurs $10 million annually should he qualify for the 30% max.

He would’ve qualified if he wins MVP, Defensive Player of the Year, or makes All-NBA next season. That possibility is now eliminated with no escalators.
